build_systems.name: Tauri 2
build_systems.notes: Bundles frontend (Vite), Rust backend, and agent-sources as app resources. Identifier: com.vibedata.skill-builder. CSP allows Anthropic API + GitHub API. Dev port: DEV_PORT (default 1420), HMR on DEV_PORT+1.

build_systems.name: Vite 8
build_systems.notes: React + Tailwind plugin. Path alias `@/` → `./src/`. E2E mock path alias via TAURI_E2E env var.

build_systems.name: GitHub Actions
build_systems.notes: pr-ci.yml: heavy platform CI (Linux/Windows/macOS) + frontend unit/integration on main push or manual dispatch. pr-policy.yml: cheap PR checks for docs build/help links, script tests, agents-structural, eval-harness, instruction-docs-lint, repo-map-audit, test-map-audit. security.yml: dependency-review and cargo-audit; GitHub code scanning provides the CodeQL PR check. docs.yml: deterministic VitePress GitHub Pages deploy. release.yml: multi-platform Tauri build, release-stage verification, and publish.

test_frameworks.name: Promptfoo
test_frameworks.scope: Agent smoke and regression tests via the root tests/evals OpenCode harness (live model calls; issue scope decides what to run)

applications.name: Skill Builder Desktop
applications.description: Desktop workflow for creating domain-specific Claude Code-compatible skills. React frontend + Rust backend + OpenHands agent runtime.

frontend_components.description: Reusable UI components. Sub-directories: agent-items/ (display item renderers: base, bare-output, error, output, result, subagent, thinking, tool, tool-activity-group), conversation/ (canonical conversation timeline + flat event-row renderer for conversation-store-backed surfaces), layout/ (app-layout, sidebar/icon-rail), settings/ (general, github, marketplace, sdk, advanced, usage sections), ui/ (shadcn/ui primitives), workspace/ (workspace-shell, workspace-conversation, workspace-overview, workspace-eval-workbench, workspace-evals, workspace-loading-skeleton, preview-panel, git-patch-view, eval-workbench shared panels, version-diff-dialog, restore-version-dialog — shown when a complete/imported/marketplace skill is selected in app-layout), step-complete/ (per-step completion views: research, detailed-research, decisions, benchmark, file-viewer + shared hooks use-step-files, use-benchmark-data). Flat components include run-status-footer, skill-list-panel (260px panel), workflow-loading-skeleton, skill cards, dialogs (delete, import, github, skill, feedback, reset, transition-gate, reconciliation-ack), clarifications-editor, agent panels, and more.

frontend_pages.description: Page-level route components: dashboard, home, settings, workflow, workspace-route

frontend_stores.description: Zustand state stores for UI/runtime state: agent-store (+ agent-display-buffer), conversation-store, settings-store, skill-store, test-store, usage-store, workflow-store, workspace-store. `conversation-store` is the canonical event transcript authority for the OpenHands conversation model migration. `skill-store` owns active skill session metadata; `workspace-store` owns workspace preview state. Server state lives under app/src/lib/queries/.

frontend_hooks.description: Custom React hooks: agent streaming, canonical conversation stream selection (`use-conversation-stream.ts`), workflow state machine, workflow session/persistence/autosave, settings form, scope blocking, marketplace registries, marketplace updates, app startup, startup dependency validation, leave guard

frontend_lib.description: Utilities, shared types, and server-state queries: tauri.ts (typed Tauri command wrappers), tauri-command-types.ts (central typed command map), tauri-command-types.typecheck.ts (compile-time command contract assertions), eval-workbench.ts (scenario and run-history helpers for the app-owned Eval Workbench), eval-run.ts (pure evaluation result helpers), eval-running-state.ts (module-scoped running-state pub/sub for app-layout gating), evals.ts (shared eval parsing helpers), query-client.ts (TanStack Query client defaults), queries/ (query keys, skills, usage, documents, plugins, auth, clarifications — useClarifications/useUpdateClarificationAnswer/useUpdateClarificationVerdicts, decisions — useDecisions, agent stream cache invalidation), types.ts (shared TS types), agent-events.ts (frontend AgentEvent types), conversation-event-types.ts + conversation-event-ordering.ts + conversation-event-projection.ts (canonical conversation event envelope, ordering, and display-node projection helpers), conversation-runtime.ts (frontend send helper for session-backed conversation actions), openhands-conversation-events.ts + openhands-event-projection.ts + openhands-result-summary.ts (OpenHands normalization and legacy display projection helpers), display-types.ts (DisplayItem and DisplayNode types), agent-results.ts (result parsing), skill-openhands-session.ts (selected-skill OpenHands bootstrap and restore hydration helper), skill-file-loader.ts (selected-skill workspace file-content loader), skill-routing.ts (getSkillSurface utility for workspace vs workflow routing), route-skill-session.ts (route/session coordinator: navigateToSkillSurface, leaveActiveSkillForNonSkillRoute — distinguishes selected-skill routing intent from active-session runtime identity), model-catalog.ts + models.ts (model metadata), workflow-steps.ts + workflow-step-configs.ts (step definitions), workflow-teardown.ts (shared workflow session teardown helper), clarifications-review.ts, clarifications-types.ts, frontmatter.ts, group-display-items.ts, path-utils.ts, app-log.ts, help-urls.ts, toast.ts, utils.ts

rust_commands.description: Tauri command handlers. Flat files: api_validation, conversation, feedback, files, git, github_auth, lifecycle, mod, model_catalog, reconciliation, runtime_lifecycle, settings, skill_session, startup, test_utils, usage, workflow_artifacts, workflow_lifecycle, workflow_session, workspace. Sub-module directories: workflow/ (step_config, deploy, output_format, guards, prompt, runtime, evaluation, settings, tests, clarifications, decisions, answer_evaluation), imported_skills/ (frontmatter, helpers, lifecycle, listing, upload), github_import/ (http, url, catalog, import, commands, updates), skill/ (crud, export, metadata, suggestions, scope_review, tests), documents/ (list_documents, list_skills_for_documents, add_document_file, add_document_url, add_document_folder, update_document, delete_document), eval_workbench/ (scenario CRUD and suggestion commands), description/ (mod.rs: run_optimization_loop/cancel/apply_description/eval-query persistence commands; loop_runner.rs: iterative improve→eval→gate loop; eval.rs: parallel eval with per-run isolated workspaces; improve.rs: Claude API description improvement)

rust_db.description: SQLite schema, migrations (code-only), and query helpers. Sub-modules: mod.rs (Db struct, init_db, pub use re-exports), migrations.rs (NUMBERED_MIGRATIONS + migration functions), skills.rs (skill CRUD/workflow runs/steps/tags + plugin CRUD: create_plugin, ensure_plugin, list_plugins, delete_plugin_by_slug, move_skill_to_plugin, plugins table management), usage.rs (persist_agent_run/usage queries), settings.rs (read/write settings), imported_skills.rs (imported skill CRUD/hash tracking), locks.rs (skill locks/check_pid_alive), workflow.rs (workflow sessions), documents.rs (document store CRUD: db_insert_document, db_list_documents, db_get_document, db_update_document_scope, db_delete_document, db_documents_for_skill), eval_workbench.rs (scenario and assertion CRUD), workflow_artifacts.rs (canonical CRUD for workflow step artifacts: upsert_clarifications, read_clarifications, upsert_decisions, read_decisions, delete_clarifications, delete_decisions, update_clarification_answer, update_clarification_verdicts), tests.rs (all DB tests).

rust_agents.description: Agent runtime. Files: openhands_server/ (Rust-managed local OpenHands Agent Server process, REST client, WebSocket event stream, persistent-session plus throwaway runtime helpers), events.rs (re-export facade for handle_runtime_message and handle_runtime_exit_with_detail), event_router.rs (routes agent_event subtypes, persists run_result, emits Tauri frontend events), event_types.rs (shared Tauri event payload types), run_persist.rs (run result persistence helpers), runtime_config.rs (runtime config builders for persistent and throwaway OpenHands runs), node_resolver.rs (Windows git-bash detection helper for runtime shell execution), skill_creator.rs (runtime config builder and persistent-session helper for skill-creator agent runs).

frontend_agent_events.description: Frontend AgentEvent type entrypoint generated from Rust contracts.

frontend_display_types.description: Canonical frontend DisplayItem definitions consumed by event projection, stores, and renderers.

agent_prompts.description: App-owned workflow prompt templates live under agent-sources/prompts/. OpenHands runtime skills for the workflow live under agent-sources/workspace/skills/ and are deployed into .agents/skills.

workspace_instructions.description: OpenHands runtime workspace sources deployed at startup. agents/ is copied to .agents/agents and skills/ is copied to .agents/skills. App prompt templates and Claude adapter templates do not live here.

app_prompts.description: App-owned prompt templates compiled/rendered by Rust and sent as explicit task messages. These files are not copied into the OpenHands runtime workspace.

rust_reconciliation.description: Startup DB↔filesystem reconciliation state machine. mod.rs (entry point: reconcile_on_startup, resolve_orphan, preview), skill_builder.rs (skill-builder skill reconciliation scenarios), marketplace.rs (marketplace skill reconciliation), tests.rs (scenario coverage tests). See docs/design/startup-recon/ for full scenario matrix.

rust_types.description: Shared Rust types split by domain. mod.rs (re-exports), settings.rs (AppSettings, MarketplaceRegistry), github.rs (GitHubUser, DeviceFlowResponse), workflow.rs (WorkflowRunRow, StepConfig, WorkflowStateResponse), skill.rs (SkillSummary, DiscoveredSkill, SkillFileEntry, SkillFileContent), session.rs (selected-skill OpenHands session restore payloads), usage.rs (UsageSummary, AgentRunRecord), startup.rs (ReconciliationResult, startup types), secret.rs (SecretString, ApiKey newtypes with redacted Debug/Display).

rust_support.description: Top-level Rust support modules (not commands or DB): cleanup.rs (future-step artifact cleanup), fs_utils.rs (canonical copy_dir_recursive with symlink guard — single implementation used by all recursive directory copies), fs_validation.rs (detect_furthest_step, has_skill_output, path validation helpers), git.rs (git commit/init helpers for skill output repos), logging.rs (structured logging setup, log-level management), marketplace_manifest.rs (write/regenerate local marketplace.json and per-plugin plugin.json for Claude Code plugin marketplace layout), skill_paths.rs (path helpers for marketplace plugin directory structure).

frontend_test_utils.description: Frontend test infrastructure: setup.ts (global Vitest setup), fixtures.ts (shared test data factories), query-test-utils.tsx (TanStack Query render helper), mocks/ (Tauri invoke mocks, store mocks, router mocks used across unit and component tests).

key_directories.app/src/: Frontend React/TypeScript runtime (components, pages, stores, hooks, lib, styles, test, router)
key_directories.app/src/styles/: Global CSS: globals.css defines Tailwind 4 base, AD brand CSS variables (--color-seafoam, --color-pacific, --color-ocean), dark-mode tokens. Source of truth for all design tokens — never use raw Tailwind palette classes.
key_directories.app/src/test/: Frontend test infrastructure: setup.ts (Vitest global setup), fixtures.ts (test data factories), mocks/ (Tauri invoke mocks, store mocks, router mocks). Distinguished from app/src/__tests__/ which contains the actual test files.
key_directories.app/src/__tests__/: Frontend unit/integration tests, mirroring src/ structure: components/, guards/, hooks/, lib/, pages/, stores/
key_directories.app/src-tauri/src/: Rust backend runtime (Tauri commands, DB, agents, types, reconciliation, support modules)
key_directories.app/e2e/: Playwright E2E tests. Sub-directories: dashboard, workflow, description, evals, settings, usage, skill-tester, skills-library, integration, helpers, setup. The description/ and evals/ specs cover the two modes of the unified Eval Workbench surface.
key_directories.tests/evals/: Root Promptfoo/OpenCode eval harness. Contains eval-map.json for coding-agent navigation, bin/ad-evals.js shared CLI, scripts/framework/ reusable harness modules, suite-owned tier config, OpenCode provider config, package-level eval configs including workbench prompt-contract packages, deterministic harness tests, static assertions for code contracts including shared Tauri command policy analysis, runtime-exported Promptfoo state ignores, one-scenario-per-package smoke, and live model regression commands.
key_directories.agent-sources/workspace/: OpenHands runtime workspace sources only: agents/ is copied to .agents/agents and skills/ is copied to .agents/skills during startup/runtime deployment.
key_directories.agent-sources/prompts/: App-owned prompt templates compiled/rendered by Rust and sent as explicit task messages; not copied into the OpenHands runtime workspace.
key_directories.docs/design/: Architecture decision records and design specs: openhands-runtime-contract, backend-design, startup-recon, clarifications-rendering, skill-tester, skills-marketplace, shared-eval-harness, and more. Primary reference for non-obvious design decisions.
key_directories.docs/plans/: Implementation plans and execution notes. Not published end-user help content.
key_directories.docs/user-guide/: VitePress user documentation, deployed via docs.yml to GitHub Pages
key_directories.docs/references/: Vendored external reference docs (e.g. Anthropic API snapshots)
key_directories..claude/rules/: Shared agent rules (agent-agnostic, detailed policy). Referenced from AGENTS.md.
key_directories.scripts/: Developer and automation scripts. worktree.sh is the canonical maintainer helper for creating development worktrees and bootstrapping app/eval dependencies. check-help-docs.mjs verifies app/VitePress help links against docs/user-guide. verify-release-stage.mjs verifies staged release archives include required runtime resources. verify-repo-map.mjs audits repo-map command/page/store inventory against the filesystem.

dependencies_internal.frontend → agent_events: Typed via app/src/lib/agent-events.ts and received as Tauri events

commands.dev_mock: cd app && npm run dev  # MOCK_AGENTS=true no longer supported; OpenHands runs through Rust-managed Agent Server
commands.test_agents_smoke: cd app && npm run test:agents:smoke  # live OpenCode/Promptfoo model call
commands.test_eval_harness_smoke: cd tests/evals && npm run eval:smoke  # one live OpenCode/Promptfoo smoke scenario per package
commands.test_eval_harness_regression: cd tests/evals && npm run eval:regression  # live OpenCode/Promptfoo package regression suite

conventions.rust: Standard Rust conventions, clippy enforced. Every Tauri command logs info! on entry, error! on failure.
conventions.commits: Granular — one concern per commit. Stage specific files (`git add <file>`, not `git add .`).
conventions.worktrees: Use ./scripts/worktree.sh <branchName>; worktrees live at ../worktrees/<branchName> relative to repo root.
conventions.linear_project: All issues for this repo go under Skill Builder
conventions.file_size_and_responsibility: Keep files focused on one concern. Split when a file exceeds ~300 lines (frontend TS/TSX) or ~400 lines (Rust command file). Rust: extract sub-module directories when a command group combines API surface, orchestration, persistence, and filesystem ops. Frontend: extract sub-components, custom hooks, or lib utilities rather than growing a component or page inline.
conventions.no_monoliths: A Tauri command file should define commands and delegate to helpers — not own query logic, filesystem ops, and business rules simultaneously. A React component should render — not own a state machine, data fetching, and complex transformations inline. When in doubt, split.

key_references.artifact_contracts: docs/design/openhands-runtime-contract/README.md — high-level runtime and artifact contract design
key_references.research_output_schema: agent-sources/workspace/skills/shared/schemas.md — canonical workflow research and answer-evaluator schema reference copied with the OpenHands workspace skills
key_references.startup_recon_design: docs/design/startup-recon/ — reconciliation scenario matrix (scenarios 1–12), used by reconciliation/
key_references.frontend_rules: .claude/rules/frontend-design.md — AD brand, component constraints, state indicators
key_references.path_conventions: .claude/rules/path-conventions.md — skill builder path vs workspace path rules
key_references.windows_compat: .claude/rules/windows-compat.md — repo-specific Windows CI guardrails for paths, CRLF, package scripts, and Rust toolchain.
key_references.logging_policy: .claude/rules/logging-policy.md — repo-specific logging rules for Tauri commands, runtime stderr, sensitive data, and file debug logs.
key_references.test_map: TEST_MAP.md — changed-path validation map, Rust command → E2E tag mappings, shared infra blast radius, cross-boundary format compliance, and live-eval boundaries.

notes_for_agents.startup: Read this file before exploring code. It is the primary startup context for structure, entrypoints, modules, and commands. Skip repo-wide rediscovery if it covers your task.
notes_for_agents.test_autonomy: `cd tests/evals && npm test` and `test:agents:structural` are deterministic. `test:agents:smoke` and `tests/evals` eval scripts make live model calls; run the smallest relevant live eval scope when an issue changes model, runtime, or eval behavior.
notes_for_agents.db_location: Database is at Tauri app_data_dir()/skill-builder.db. macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/com.vibedata.skill-builder/skill-builder.db
notes_for_agents.migrations: DB migrations are code-only in db/migrations.rs NUMBERED_MIGRATIONS constant — no separate migration files directory.
notes_for_agents.workspace_path: Workspace: <app_local_data_dir>/workspace (not user-configurable). Resolved from Tauri app_local_data_dir() by resolve_workspace_path() in commands/workspace.rs. Legacy ~/.vibedata/ path is cleaned up automatically on startup.
notes_for_agents.mock_mode: MOCK_AGENTS=true is no longer supported — the legacy mock chain has been removed. OpenHands runs through the Rust-managed Agent Server.
notes_for_agents.agent_tests_changed: When agents/*.md or agent-sources/workspace/** changes, run `cd app && npm run test:agents:structural` before reporting completion.
notes_for_agents.sdk_limitations: See AGENTS.md Gotchas — SDK has no team tools (no TeamCreate/TaskCreate/SendMessage). Use the Task tool for sub-agents; multiple Task calls in the same turn run in parallel.
notes_for_agents.pre_commit_checks: See AGENTS.md Issue Management for the full pre-commit checklist (markdownlint, tsc, cargo check, lint-agent-docs).
notes_for_agents.test_selection: For Rust or cross-layer changes, read TEST_MAP.md to find the correct cargo filter and E2E tag before running tests. Frontend test selection is handled automatically by `vitest --changed`.
notes_for_agents.path_safety: All filesystem operations on user-supplied skill names must call validate_skill_name() and use fs::canonicalize + starts_with before any remove_dir_all or rename. See commands/skill/crud.rs delete_skill_inner for the canonical pattern.
notes_for_agents.update_repo_map: Refresh this file whenever you change architecture, entrypoints, commands, modules, or package structure.
